location.origin: Born June 19, 1950, in San Diego, California. Father John Wilson was a Major (later Colonel) in the U.S. Marine Corps who led the Marine Corps band. Mother Lou Wilson was a concert pianist and choir singer. Family moved frequently to military bases in Panama and Taiwan before settling in Bellevue, Washington, in the early 1960s. Father taught English at Sammamish High School after retiring from the Marines. Family lived on 166th Avenue in Bellevue. Ann graduated from Sammamish High School in 1968. Attended Cornish College of the Arts in Seattle. Shy, stuttered as a child, found expression through music. Household musical diet: opera, Ray Charles, Judy Garland, Peggy Lee, bossa nova, early experimental electronic music -- 'pancakes and opera' on Sunday mornings.
location.formative: Bellevue/Seattle, Washington (1960s-1970s): After high school, sang with Seattle-area bar bands. In 1970-71, answered a newspaper ad from White Heart (Roger Fisher, Steve Fossen) seeking a singer -- band had evolved from The Army (1967-69) and Hocus Pocus (1969-70). Renamed Heart in April 1972. Relationship with Michael Fisher (Roger's brother, Heart's manager) led band to relocate to Vancouver, BC, where Michael had gone to avoid Vietnam draft. Sister Nancy Wilson joined Heart in 1974. Recorded debut Dreamboat Annie at Mushroom Studios, Vancouver (1975). Heart always identified as a Seattle band despite Vancouver recording base. Cornish College of the Arts connection. Eastside/Bellevue suburban geography feeding into Seattle music scene.
location.career: Seattle/Bellevue, Washington (career base, 1970-present). Heart discography: Dreamboat Annie (1975, Mushroom -- #7 Billboard 200, multi-platinum, 'Magic Man' #9 Hot 100, 'Crazy on You' #35), Magazine (1977, Mushroom), Little Queen (1977, Portrait -- #9 Billboard 200, platinum, 'Barracuda' #11 Hot 100), Dog & Butterfly (1978, Portrait -- #17, platinum), Bebe le Strange (1980, Epic -- #5, platinum), Private Audition (1982, Epic), Passionworks (1983, Epic), Heart (1985, Capitol -- #1 Billboard 200, quintuple platinum, 'What About Love' #10, 'Never' #4, 'These Dreams' #1, 'Nothin' at All' #10), Bad Animals (1987, Capitol -- #2, triple platinum, 'Alone' #1 for 3 weeks), Brigade (1990, Capitol -- #3, triple platinum, 'All I Wanna Do Is Make Love to You' #2), Desire Walks On (1993, Capitol -- #48), Jupiter's Darling (2004, Sovereign Artists), Red Velvet Car (2010, Legacy -- #10), Fanatic (2012, Legacy), Beautiful Broken (2016, Concord). Solo career: Hope & Glory (2007, Rounder/Zoe -- covers album with Elton John, k.d. lang, Alison Krauss, Wynonna Judd), The Ann Wilson Thing! EPs (2015-2016, digital), Immortal (2018, BMG -- covers/tribute to departed artists: Bowie, Winehouse, Petty, Chris Cornell), Fierce Bliss (2022, Silver Lining Music -- first solo album with original material, guests Vince Gill, Kenny Wayne Shepherd, Warren Haynes), Another Door (2023, LouJon Records -- with band Tripsitter, first album as sole lyricist on all tracks). Heart total: 15 studio albums, 35-50 million records worldwide, 22.5 million certified US albums, 29 Billboard Hot 100 singles, 2 #1 singles, top-10 albums across 4 decades (1970s-2010s). Awards: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame (2013), Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award (2023), Hollywood Walk of Fame (2012), Kennedy Center Honoree (2024), 4 Grammy nominations (no competitive wins). Cancer diagnosis July 2024, surgery and chemotherapy, resumed touring February 2025. Voice classified as mezzo-soprano, approximately 4-octave range. Often compared to Robert Plant. Adopted daughter Marie (1991) and son Dustin (1998). Married Dean Wetter (April 2015). Seattle connection is foundational: Bellevue childhood, Seattle bar bands, Heart formed from Eastside musicians, always identified as Seattle band.

labels: Portrait Records (Heart: Little Queen 1977, Dog & Butterfly 1978)
Epic Records (Heart: Bebe le Strange 1980, Private Audition 1982, Passionworks 1983)
Capitol Records (Heart: Heart 1985, Bad Animals 1987, Brigade 1990, Desire Walks On 1993)
Legacy Recordings/Concord (Heart: Red Velvet Car 2010, Fanatic 2012, Beautiful Broken 2016)

members.name: Ann Wilson
members.role: Lead vocals, flute, songwriter (Heart co-founder + solo)
members.note: Born Ann Dustin Wilson, June 19, 1950, San Diego, California. Mezzo-soprano, approximately 4-octave range. Sammamish High School 1968. Cornish College of the Arts. Joined White Heart (later Heart) 1970-71. Rock and Roll Hall of Fame 2013. Grammy Lifetime Achievement 2023. Hollywood Walk of Fame 2012. Kennedy Center Honoree 2024. Cancer diagnosis and treatment 2024, resumed touring 2025. Adopted daughter Marie (1991), adopted son Dustin (1998). Married Dean Wetter (2015).

associated_musicians.name: Nancy Wilson
associated_musicians.note: Born March 16, 1954, San Francisco, California. Raised in Bellevue, Washington. Joined Heart in 1974. Rhythm and acoustic guitar, backing and occasional lead vocals ('These Dreams' #1). Co-songwriter with Ann on many Heart songs. Rock and Roll Hall of Fame 2013. Grammy Lifetime Achievement 2023. Film scoring work including Almost Famous (2000, Cameron Crowe -- her then-husband). The sister axis defined Heart's identity for fifty years.

associated_musicians.name: Roger Fisher
associated_musicians.note: Co-founded The Army (1967), Hocus Pocus, White Heart, and Heart with Steve Fossen. Placed the newspaper ad Ann Wilson answered. Left Heart in 1980. Reunited for Rock Hall induction 2013.

associated_musicians.name: Steve Fossen
associated_musicians.note: Co-founded The Army, Hocus Pocus, White Heart, and Heart with Roger Fisher. Left Heart in 1982. Reunited for Rock Hall induction 2013.

associated_musicians.name: Michael Fisher
associated_musicians.note: Roger Fisher's brother. Ann Wilson's romantic partner throughout the 1970s. Relocated to Vancouver to avoid Vietnam draft, drawing Heart to Canada. Managed Heart during breakthrough era. Relationship with Ann ended 1979. 'Magic Man' is about him.

associated_musicians.name: Howard Leese
associated_musicians.note: Long-serving member through Heart's classic era. Studio and touring guitarist across the 1970s and 1980s commercial peak.

associated_musicians.name: Chris Cornell
associated_musicians.note: Soundgarden/Audioslave frontman. Wilson covered his 'I Am the Highway' on Immortal (2018) as tribute after Cornell's death by suicide May 18, 2017. Seattle voice carrying Seattle voice.

associated_musicians.name: Vince Gill
associated_musicians.note: Country legend. Guest appearance on Wilson's first solo album with original material.

associated_musicians.name: Kenny Wayne Shepherd
associated_musicians.note: Blues-rock guitarist. Guest appearance bridging Wilson's rock identity to contemporary blues-rock.

associated_musicians.name: Warren Haynes
associated_musicians.note: Allman Brothers Band/Gov't Mule guitarist. Guest appearance connecting Wilson to Southern rock tradition.

theory_nodes.concept: The radiance (forty-fourth acoustic element -- the luminous overtone field generated when a voice is projected at such power and precision that the harmonic series becomes audible as a distinct sonic layer above the fundamental pitch; not vibrato, not tremolo, but the radiance of a voice that excites the room's acoustics while maintaining coherent overtones)
examples: Ann Wilson (degree 43) -- sustained notes in 'Crazy on You,' 'Alone,' and 'Barracuda' produce a radiance: the fundamental pitch is the center, and the overtone series creates a luminous halo around it. The mezzo-soprano range combined with rock-level projection excites room acoustics into coherent harmonic resonance.

Contrast with the altar call (degree 42, Erica Campbell) -- the voice that summons participation. The radiance needs no congregation; it fills the space whether anyone is listening or not. The altar call is directed at others; the radiance is self-sustaining.
Contrast with the whiskey falsetto (degree 41, Shawn Smith) -- intimate, interior. The radiance is expansive, room-filling. Opposite ends of the vocal power spectrum.

The radiance bridges the engine's soul/gospel sector and rock sector: soul radiances through melisma and emotional bending; rock radiances through power and sustain. Wilson's Rock/Soul genre compound produces a radiance containing both.
theory_nodes.description: The radiance is the forty-fourth acoustic element in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the vocal phenomenon in which sustained power creates a luminous overtone field -- a halo of harmonic energy surrounding the primary note. The radiance is not vibrato (periodic pitch oscillation) and not tremolo (amplitude variation). It is what happens when a voice is projected at such power and precision that the overtone series becomes audible as a distinct sonic layer above the fundamental pitch. Ann Wilson's mezzo-soprano, trained in a household of opera and Ray Charles, delivered at rock volume, produces the radiance in its purest form. The voice does not simply hold the note -- it radiates. The radiance is the culmination concept of the acoustic progression: the voice that transcends the room not through invitation (altar call) but through sheer acoustic force.

ancestors: The altar call (degree 42, Erica Campbell) -- the voice that summons. The radiance is the voice that fills without summoning.
The whiskey falsetto (degree 41, Shawn Smith) -- intimate, self-directed. The radiance is expansive, space-directed. Opposite orientations.
The sonic boom (degree 36, Jimi Hendrix) -- instrument-generated acoustic event. The radiance is voice-generated. The E=6 pair: guitar boom and vocal radiance.
genealogy.relationship: Forty-four acoustic elements across forty-four degrees. One per degree, no exceptions. The radiance is the engine's first element describing the physics of vocal projection at rock volume -- the overtone phenomenon that distinguishes great rock vocalists from adequate ones. Track element 45 at degree 44.

theory_nodes.concept: The lifer (forty-fourth artist-city pattern -- an artist whose connection to a city spans the entire career arc: childhood arrival, formative years, career formation, commercial peak, commercial decline, recovery, late career, all mapped to the same city; the city is not a chapter in the story but the whole book)
examples: Ann Wilson (degree 43) -- arrived in Bellevue as a child in the early 1960s, graduated Sammamish High School 1968, joined Seattle bar bands, formed Heart from Seattle-area musicians, recorded in Vancouver but remained a Seattle band in identity, achieved global fame identified as 'from Seattle,' survived grunge (Seattle's own revolution), and is still performing fifty-five years later. The entire arc mapped to one metropolitan area.
Contrast with the circuit rider (degree 42, Erica Campbell) -- periodic visits through institutional networks. The lifer is permanent presence. The circuit rider arrives and departs; the lifer stays.

Contrast with the deep root (degree 36, Jimi Hendrix) -- deep connection but brief duration. Hendrix's Seattle years were childhood; he left at 18 and never returned to live. Wilson arrived as a child and stayed for life.
Contrast with the export (degree 0, Quincy Jones) -- left the city permanently. The lifer is the anti-export: the artist who stays.
theory_nodes.description: The lifer is the forty-fourth artist-city pattern in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes an artist whose connection to a city is not a phase, a chapter, or a springboard, but the entire book. The lifer arrived (or was brought) as a child, formed their identity in the city's musical ecosystem, built their career from its stages and studios, left and returned, survived the city's own musical revolutions, and at the end of a multi-decade career is still identified with the city. Ann Wilson's family settled in Bellevue in the early 1960s. She formed Heart from Seattle-area bands. Heart was always a Seattle band. When Seattle's grunge movement demolished 1980s arena rock, Wilson -- a Seattle artist -- absorbed the blow and survived. Fifty-five years later, she is still performing, still identified with Seattle. The lifer carries a structural irony: the city that made Wilson nearly unmade her through its own next generation.

ancestors: The circuit rider (degree 42, Erica Campbell) -- periodic presence through networks. The lifer is permanent presence through life commitment.
The scene native (degree 41, Shawn Smith) -- geographic presence without genre alignment. The lifer has both geography AND genre alignment, sustained across decades.
The deep root (degree 36, Jimi Hendrix) -- deep but brief. The lifer is deep AND long.
genealogy.relationship: Forty-four artist-city patterns across forty-four degrees. The lifer is the engine's most comprehensive city-connection pattern: it encompasses arrival, formation, peak, decline, survival, and late career -- the entire arc. Track pattern 45 at degree 44.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the power bend (forty-third deployment; the blue note delivered at full rock volume, sustained and projected, filling the room with physical force; the complement to the sanctified bend (42) which bent upward into transcendence -- the power bend radiates outward into kinetic energy)
examples: Ann Wilson (degree 43) -- 'Barracuda' vocal attack bends notes with physical force. 'Alone' sustains blue-note-tinged vowels at full volume for beats that feel impossible. The power bend is the blue note scaled up from jazz intimacy to rock arena dimensions.

Contrast with the sanctified bend (degree 42, Erica Campbell) -- bends upward into spiritual transcendence. The power bend radiates outward into physical force. Consecutive deployments, opposite vectors: one ascends, one radiates.
Contrast with the interior blue (degree 41, Shawn Smith) -- inward-directed. The power bend is outward-directed at maximum amplitude.
Connection to the sonic boom (degree 36, Jimi Hendrix) -- instrument-level power. The power bend applies the same kinetic principle to the voice. The E=6 pair: guitar power and vocal power.
theory_nodes.description: The power bend is the forty-third blue note deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the blue note delivered not as inflection or suggestion but as a sustained, full-volume vocal event. The conventional blue note is subtle -- a shadow falling across the melody. The power bend is the blue note at maximum amplitude: the flatted third or seventh sung at full rock projection, held long enough and loud enough to function as a physical force in the room. Ann Wilson's vocal performances are defined by these moments. The power bend is what happens when a voice trained in the blues tradition (the Wilson household's Ray Charles records) operates at the volume of hard rock. It is the blues note scaled to arena dimensions.

ancestors: Blue note -- the sanctified bend (degree 42, Erica Campbell): upward vector. The power bend is outward vector.
Blue note -- the interior blue (degree 41, Shawn Smith): inward containment. The power bend is outward projection.
All forty-two prior blue note deployments (degrees 0-42)
genealogy.relationship: Forty-three deployments across forty-four degrees. The power bend follows the sanctified bend (42) -- spiritual ascent followed by physical radiation. The sanctified bend transforms you spiritually; the power bend hits you physically. Track deployment 44 at degree 44.

theory_nodes.concept: E=6 twin peak mode (second E=6 deployment -- ties Jimi Hendrix as maximum energy; both Seattle, both Rock, both transformed their instrument; distribution shifts to 2:13:7:12:9:2 with E=1 and E=6 symmetrical at 2; the engine's only E=6 pair, seven degrees apart)
examples: Ann Wilson (degree 43) -- E=6. 35-50 million records sold (Heart). 29 Billboard Hot 100 singles. Two #1 singles. #1 album. Rock and Roll Hall of Fame. Grammy Lifetime Achievement. Kennedy Center Honoree. Top-10 albums across four decades. Cancer survivor still performing at 74. E=6 measures the voice that never diminished across fifty-five years.

Jimi Hendrix (degree 36) -- E=6. The only other maximum energy. Both Seattle. Both Rock. Hendrix: supernova (4 years, transcendent). Wilson: sustained output (55+ years, resilient). The twin peaks: brief impossible intensity and long impossible endurance.
Distribution after degree 43: 2:13:7:12:9:2 (E=1:E=2:E=3:E=4:E=5:E=6). E=1 and E=6 now both at 2, creating bookend symmetry. The extremes match.
No jazz, soul, R&B, gospel, or funk artist has reached E=6. Only Rock (broadly defined) produces E=6 in this engine. Both E=6 artists broke barriers: Hendrix (racial), Wilson (gender).
theory_nodes.description: E=6 twin peak mode is the second E=6 deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. It registers the arrival of Ann Wilson at the maximum energy level, tying Jimi Hendrix (degree 36) as the only other E=6 artist. The twin peaks share three structural attributes: both from Seattle, both in the Rock tradition, both transformed their primary instrument (guitar, voice). The energy distribution shifts to 2:13:7:12:9:2, with E=1 and E=6 creating bookend symmetry at 2 each. The E=6 pair stands seven degrees apart (36-43), both in the rock-adjacent quadrant of the engine. Wilson's E=6 measures fifty-five years of sustained maximum output: 35-50 million records, two #1 singles, Rock Hall, Grammy Lifetime Achievement, Kennedy Center Honors, cancer survival, ongoing performance.

ancestors: E=6 supernova mode (degree 36, Jimi Hendrix, E=6) -- the first E=6. Brief, transcendent. Twin peak mode is sustained, resilient.
E=5 return mode (degree 42, Erica Campbell, E=5) -- E=5 followed by E=6. The energy ramps from the gospel return to the rock peak.
E=4 interior mode (degree 41, Shawn Smith, E=4) -- recalibration. Three degrees later, the engine reaches its ceiling again.
genealogy.relationship: Two E=6 modes across forty-four degrees. The E=6 pair is the engine's rarest energy classification. Distribution: 2:13:7:12:9:2. E=1/E=6 symmetry at 2. Only Rock produces E=6. Only Seattle produces E=6. Track at degree 44.

theory_nodes.concept: Rock/Soul genre stage (Genre Stage 24 -- the first rock-primary genre stage in the engine; rock as primary genre element for the first time in forty-four degrees; the soul qualifier maps the vocal inheritance from a household of opera and Ray Charles; the genre map reaches its most commercially dominant tradition)
examples: Ann Wilson (degree 43) -- Rock/Soul. Rock primary (Heart: hard rock, arena rock, power ballad, 15 studio albums, 35-50M sales). Soul secondary (vocal delivery shaped by Ray Charles records in the Wilson household, mezzo-soprano training, emotional bending in rock framework).
Genre evolution: Gospel/R&B (42) -> Rock/Soul (43). The engine moves from sacred music to rock for the first time. Twenty-four genre stages, and rock appears as primary only now.

Blues Rock appeared at degrees 36-37 (Hendrix, Jones), but blues was the primary element. Rock/Soul puts rock first. The engine's genre map reaches the tradition that has dominated global commercial music since the 1960s.

The soul qualifier is essential: Wilson does not sing rock like a machine. She sings rock like someone who grew up hearing Ray Charles. The vocal bend, the emotional content, the radiance -- all carry soul DNA into rock delivery.
theory_nodes.description: Rock/Soul at degree 43 is Genre Stage 24 in the Seattle 360 engine -- the first time rock appears as a primary genre element. The engine has spent forty-three degrees documenting jazz, soul, funk, R&B, blues, neo-soul, blues rock, and gospel. At degree 43, rock steps forward as the primary genre. Blues Rock (degrees 36-37) placed blues as primary; Rock/Soul places rock as primary. This is a watershed: the genre map reaches the tradition that has dominated global commercial music since the mid-1960s. The soul qualifier acknowledges the vocal inheritance: Ann Wilson's rock power sits on a foundation of soul phrasing, shaped by the Ray Charles and Judy Garland records that played in the Wilson household alongside opera. Rock/Soul is not a compromise; it is the truth of the voice.

ancestors: Gospel/R&B (degree 42, Erica Campbell) -- Genre Stage 23. Sacred music. Rock/Soul is the secular rock counterpart.
Soul/Blues (degree 41, Shawn Smith) -- Genre Stage 22. Soul primary. Rock/Soul shifts the primary from soul to rock.
Blues Rock (degree 36-37, Hendrix/Jones) -- Blues primary with rock modifier. Rock/Soul reverses: rock primary with soul modifier.
genealogy.relationship: Twenty-four genre stages across forty-four degrees. Rock/Soul is the first rock-primary stage. The genre map now includes rock as a primary element for the first time. The evolution from Blues (35) through Blues Rock (36-37) to Rock/Soul (43) traces the genre shift across nine degrees. Track at degree 44.

seattle_connection: THE LIFER -- Ann Wilson (degree 43) connects to Seattle as a lifelong resident and artist. Born San Diego; family settled in Bellevue, WA, early 1960s. Graduated Sammamish High School 1968. Sang in Seattle bar bands. Answered newspaper ad from Seattle-area band White Heart (later Heart) 1970-71. Heart formed from Eastside musicians (Roger Fisher, Steve Fossen). Recorded in Vancouver but always identified as a Seattle band. Survived grunge -- Seattle's own revolution that demolished 1980s arena rock. Still performing fifty-five years later. SECOND E=6 in the engine -- ties Jimi Hendrix (36). Both Seattle. Both Rock. Both transformed their instrument. Only E=6 pair. FIRST ROCK-PRIMARY GENRE STAGE at Genre Stage 24. THE RADIANCE as 44th acoustic element -- power-generated overtone field, mezzo-soprano at rock projection. THE LIFER as 44th artist-city pattern -- entire career arc mapped to one city. THE POWER BEND as 43rd blue note -- blue note at rock volume. NINTH WOMAN -- gender ratio crosses 20% for first time (9/44 = 20.5%). Three women in four degrees (40, 42, 43). E=6 distribution: 2:13:7:12:9:2. E=1/E=6 bookend symmetry at 2. Heart: 15 studio albums, 35-50M records, 29 Hot 100 singles, 2 #1s, #1 album, quintuple platinum self-titled (1985). Solo: Hope & Glory (2007), Immortal (2018, BMG), Fierce Bliss (2022, Silver Lining), Another Door (2023, LouJon). Rock and Roll Hall of Fame 2013. Grammy Lifetime Achievement 2023. Hollywood Walk of Fame 2012. Kennedy Center Honoree 2024. Cancer diagnosis 2024, resumed touring 2025. Voice: mezzo-soprano, ~4 octaves. Often compared to Robert Plant. Robert Plant wept during Heart's 'Stairway to Heaven' at 2012 Kennedy Center Honors. Chris Cornell tribute on Immortal. The E=6 pair: guitar (Hendrix) and voice (Wilson). Both Seattle. Both Rock. The twin peaks.

theory_genealogy: The radiance (NEW at degree 43): 44th acoustic element. Power-generated overtone field. Voice at such power and precision that overtone series becomes audible. Rock vocal radiance. Track element 45 at degree 44.
The lifer (NEW at degree 43): 44th artist-city pattern. Entire career arc mapped to one city -- childhood through late career. First full-arc pattern. Track pattern 45 at degree 44.
Blue note -- the power bend (NEW at degree 43): 43rd deployment. Blue note at full rock volume. Outward radiation vs sanctified bend's upward ascent. Track deployment 44 at degree 44.
E=6 twin peak mode (NEW at degree 43): 2nd E=6 deployment. Ties Hendrix. Distribution 2:13:7:12:9:2. Bookend symmetry. Track at degree 44.
Rock/Soul genre stage (NEW at degree 43): Genre Stage 24. First rock-primary stage. Rock enters the genre map as primary element. Track at degree 44.
engine_sequencing: E=6 RETURNS at 43: Distribution 2:13:7:12:9:2. Twin peak mode as 2nd E=6. Seven degrees after Hendrix (36). Both Seattle. Both Rock. E=1/E=6 symmetry at 2. Track at degree 44.
GENRE Stage 24 at degree 43: Rock/Soul. First rock-primary stage. The genre map reaches rock as primary element for the first time. Track at degree 44.
FIRST ROCK-PRIMARY at degree 43: Genre expansion landmark. Rock primary after 43 degrees of jazz, soul, funk, R&B, blues, neo-soul, gospel. Track rock thread.
SEATTLE returns at degree 43: After Tacoma at 42. Wilson's Seattle is the entire metropolitan area -- Bellevue childhood through Seattle career.
THE RADIANCE as 44th acoustic element: Power-generated overtone field. Mezzo-soprano at rock projection. Track element 45 at degree 44.
THE LIFER as 44th artist-city pattern: Entire career arc mapped to one city. First full-arc pattern. Track at degree 44.
THE POWER BEND as 43rd blue note: Blue note at rock volume. Outward radiation. Track deployment 44 at degree 44.
BMG label at degree 43: Breaks the Columbia/Sony streak (40-42). Solo career on independent/mid-major labels. Track label thread.
NINTH WOMAN at degree 43: Gender ratio 9:35 (~20.5%). Crosses 20% threshold. Three women in four degrees (40, 42, 43) -- densest female representation. Track.
CANCER SURVIVOR still performing: E=6 does not diminish. Track health/resilience thread.
All engine sequencing items from degrees 0-42
cultural_patterns: Artist-city taxonomy: FORTY-FOUR named patterns (adding The Lifer at degree 43). First full-arc pattern. Track.
Gender ratio: 9:35 (women:men). ~20.5%. Crosses 20% threshold. Three women in four degrees (40, 42, 43). Track.
ROCK enters the engine as primary genre: First rock-primary stage (Genre Stage 24). Rock has been modifier (Blues Rock 36-37) but never primary until now. Track rock thread.
E=6 PAIR: Hendrix (36) and Wilson (43). Both Seattle. Both Rock. Both barrier-breakers (racial, gender). Only E=6 pair. Track.
SEATTLE's E=6 monopoly: Only city to produce E=6 artists. Two of them. Track whether E=6 appears outside Seattle.
WILSON HOUSEHOLD as origin: Opera + Ray Charles + Judy Garland + Peggy Lee + bossa nova + experimental electronic. The most ecumenical musical household in the engine. Track.
CHRIS CORNELL tribute on Immortal: Seattle voice carrying Seattle voice. Wilson covered 'I Am the Highway' after Cornell's 2017 death. Track Seattle community thread.
GRUNGE survival: Seattle's own revolution nearly ended Wilson's career. The lifer survived the city's own next generation. Track.
HEART's 50-year span: Top-10 albums across four decades (1970s-2010s). Matched by very few rock acts. Track longevity thread.
KENNEDY CENTER HONORS 2024: Heart as honorees. Wilson and Nancy previously performed there for Led Zeppelin (2012). Track.
All cultural pattern items from degrees 0-42
